product.name: Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot, NYUMA (Taypro)
product.description: Taypro NYUMA is a fully autonomous Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot for utility-scale solar power plants. Waterless AI- and ML-driven single-pass dry cleaning removes over 99% of dust per cycle, cleans up to 3,600 modules on a single charge, connects to NECTYR via LTE, Wi-Fi, hybrid self-healing RF mesh, LoRa, and LoRaWAN, and is field-validated and TÜV NORD certified.

overview.eyebrow: Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot for Utility-Scale Plants
overview.title: What is the Taypro NYUMA Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot?
overview.p1AfterCategory: category describes fully autonomous, waterless robots that clean solar modules without tankers or night crews. Taypro NYUMA is a fully autonomous unit engineered for reliable daily cleaning of utility-scale solar power plants. A single automated run removes over
overview.p1AfterDustClaim: from the module surface using a rotating, self-cleaning PBT brush drum, driving consistent performance ratio gains across the plant (site-dependent).

methodologyNotice.body: References to "99%+" on this site describe dust removed from the module surface per automated pass or cycle, not a guarantee of panel conversion efficiency or total plant output. Recovery of energy lost to soiling depends on baseline soiling, cleaning cadence, weather, and plant design. See our performance & test methodology for how Taypro validates and quotes results.

overview.p2BeforeStrong: On a single charge, NYUMA cleans up to
overview.p2Strong: 2.2 km of running length, approximately 3,600 solar modules
overview.p2AfterStrong: . Cleaning cycles are best scheduled outside energy production hours and can be programmed remotely, ensuring zero impact on plant generation.
overview.p3: NYUMA's counter-rotating PBT brush drum lifts dry dust in a single waterless pass — no airflow stage, fewer consumables, and straightforward brush replacement in the field. Edge detection and surface-undulation tracking keep contact on the module frame, never the glass. Battery-aware routing ensures each robot finishes its assigned row before returning to its shadow-free dock, even on seasonal-tilt tables up to 45°.

howToSection.title: How Does an Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot Work?
howToSection.subtitle: NYUMA executes autonomous single-pass PBT cleaning without row crews. The workflow below mirrors our HowTo structured data and the nightly cycle run at NYUMA automatic fleets.

step0.name: Assign each Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot to a dedicated array
step0.text: Each NYUMA unit is assigned to one array with a dedicated dock and self-contained lithium-ion charging — PBT brush calibration and frame pairing happen once at commissioning.

step1.name: Schedule cleaning from the NECTYR with AI- and ML-driven logic
step1.text: Operators schedule NYUMA through NECTYR. Weather-aware logic skips rain-washed days and prioritises post-storm PBT runs when dust rebound is highest.

step2.name: Robot self-deploys onto the module row
step2.text: NYUMA self-deploys along the module frame at the scheduled time — edge and obstacle mapping keeps the PBT drum on the frame, never on the glass.

step3.name: single-pass waterless cleaning with a self-cleaning PBT brush drum
step3.text: A rotating, self-cleaning PBT brush drum executes a single-pass dry cleaning cycle that removes over 99% of accumulated dust per automated run at 10–15 metres per minute, with zero water, detergent or manual labour.

step4.name: Battery-aware return to the docking station
step4.text: Real-time battery monitoring caps each run to a completable distance; NYUMA returns to dock and locks against gusts up to 180 km/hr before the next single-pass cycle.

step5.name: Sync telemetry and fleet health to the cloud
step5.textBeforeConnectivity: Single-pass cycle logs — brush hours, distance, charge state — upload to NECTYR over
step5.textAfterConnectivity: , pick the link that best fits each site, for audit-ready reporting across every deployed Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot.

servicePromise.title: Service & Maintenance Promise

card0.title: Scheduled Preventive Maintenance
card0.body: NYUMA AMC includes brush-hour tracking, scheduled PBT cartridge inspection, and spare planning aligned to your site's dust loading.

card1.title: Immediate Remote Diagnostics
card1.body: Most NYUMA incidents are triaged remotely via NECTYR — firmware, schedule, and telemetry fixes land over-the-air before a truck roll.

card2.title: Same-Day On-Site Intervention

featuresLongForm.mainHeading: Features of Taypro's Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robots: NYUMA

aiCleaning.title: AI-Enabled Waterless Single-Pass Cleaning
aiCleaning.body: NYUMA applies AI- and ML-driven pressure and speed logic to a single-pass PBT brush drum — one continuous contact sweep removes over 99% of dry dust per automated run without water, detergent, or abrasive glass contact.

microfiber.title: Self-Cleaning PBT Brush Drum
microfiber.body: UV-stable PBT bristles maintain stiffness in desert heat while a self-cleaning drum mechanism clears embedded dust between modules — consumable swaps are field-serviceable without removing the robot from the row.

range.title: Extended Cleaning Range
range.body: NYUMA cleans up to 2.2 km of running length, roughly 3,600 modules, on a single charge, at cleaning speeds of 10–15 m/min. With its lithium-ion battery and integrated charging system, each robot is assigned to a specific array and is deployed quickly without additional railings, across a wide range of panel inclinations and seasonal tilts.

battery.title: Battery-Aware Autonomy
battery.body: NYUMA monitors battery level in real time and only commits to a cleaning run it can complete, safely returning to its docking station every cycle. The robot stays securely locked when docked and is designed to withstand wind gusts of up to 180 km/hr at the docking position.

weather.title: Smart Weather Optimization
weather.body: NYUMA's scheduler skips cycles when rain has already washed modules and adds post-storm runs when dust rebound is detected — optimising PBT brush life while keeping PR stable.

connectivity.title: Cloud-Connected Remote Monitoring
connectivity.bodyBeforeStrong: Every NYUMA robot connects to the NECTYR using
connectivity.bodyAfterConnectivityStrong: . Deployments can use LTE or Wi-Fi for high-bandwidth telemetry, a
connectivity.bodyAfterLoRa: where low-power, long-range links suit remote blocks. Operate mixed NYUMA fleets from NECTYR — schedule single-pass cycles, track brush-hour counters, and download audit logs for O&M governance.

edge.title: Edge & Obstacle Detection Technology
edge.body: Edge-mapping sensors and obstacle detection keep NYUMA on the frame during single-pass runs at 10–15 m/min — essential on seasonal-tilt tables with uneven undulation.

build.title: Sealed, Field-Hardened Build
build.body: Sealed IP65 electronics and C3 corrosion protection shield the PBT drivetrain from abrasive dust ingress — validated for Indian damp-heat and dry-heat cycles.

certifications.title: Certifications & Field-Validated Testing
certifications.subtitle: Every Taypro NYUMA is subjected to rigorous laboratory and field validation by accredited solar-sector testing laboratories, simulating real plant conditions to guarantee long-term reliability.

card0.title: TÜV NORD Certified
card0.body: Independently tested and certified for IP55 protection and validated for extreme damp-heat and dry-heat performance by TÜV NORD.

card1.title: Sandstorm-Endurance Tested
card1.body: Validated under simulated outdoor cleaning cycles, including 12 sandstorm events per year at a sand loading of 10 g/m² per cycle.

card2.title: Panel-Safe Cleaning
card2.body: Testing covers micro-crack analysis, optical reflectance measurements, full electrical parameter evaluation and Anti-Reflective Coating (ARC) preservation.

manualVsAutomatic.title: Why Choose an Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot Over Manual Cleaning?
manualVsAutomatic.subtitle: Manual cleaning crews, water tankers and ad-hoc schedules cannot keep up with the daily soiling losses on utility-scale solar power plants. An automatic robotic solar cleaning solution is no longer a premium, it's a performance-ratio essential.

row1.manual: High, 1.5 to 3 litres of water per module per wash
row1.robot: Zero, fully waterless single-pass dry cleaning

row2.robot: 99%+ dust removal in a single automated run, every time

row3.manual: High labour cost, panel damage risk, night-shift safety risk
row3.robot: Autonomous after-hours operation, no manual intervention

row4.manual: Soiling losses of 5–25% accumulate between washes
row4.robot: Daily cleaning sustains peak PR, lifting plant yield 4–8%

row5.manual: Risk of micro-cracks, ARC abrasion and scratches
row5.robot: Robot rides on the module frame, no load on glass or cells

row6.manual: No telemetry, no cleaning audit trail

row7.manual: Recurring water, labour, equipment and downtime costs
row7.robot: One-time investment, 12–18 month payback, 20-year design life

indianConditions.title: Built for Indian Utility-Scale Solar Plants
indianConditions.subtitle: Rajasthan dust storms, Gujarat heat, Karnataka humidity and Tamil Nadu monsoons, Taypro NYUMA is engineered, tested and deployed for the realities of large-scale Indian solar O&M, not generic Western field conditions.

card0.title: High-Soiling Region Performance
card0.body: NYUMA automatic fleets serve high-soiling belts — Akhadana-scale deployments show how PBT robots sustain cadence when wet washing cannot keep pace.

card1.title: Temperature & Humidity Tolerance
card1.body: Validated for operating temperatures up to 90°C and certified by TÜV NORD for damp-heat and dry-heat performance, proven across Indian summer, monsoon and post-monsoon cycles.

card2.title: Waterless, Even in Water-Scarce States
card2.body: Single-pass dry cleaning eliminates tanker dependency — critical where state water boards restrict module washing allocations.

card3.title: Pan-India Service Network
card3.body: Same-day on-site breakdown resolution across India, with immediate remote diagnostics from NECTYR, backed by regional spare inventory and structured AMC programs.

advantagesSection.heading: Advantages of Using Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robots

energy.title: Increased Energy Efficiency
energy.body: Consistent single-pass PBT cleaning prevents the 5–25% soiling drift common between manual washes — owners often model 4–8% PR uplift on high-soiling sites.

waterless.title: Waterless Cleaning
waterless.body: NYUMA removes dust with a dry PBT brush only — zero litres per module, no tanker logistics, and no discharge risk in water-scarce districts.

autonomous.title: Fully Autonomous & Smart Operation
autonomous.body: NYUMA deploys autonomously after a one-time dock install — operators manage cadence remotely in NECTYR without night crews on the row.

cost.title: Cost-Effective Solution
cost.body: Single-pass PBT economics reduce upfront consumable complexity versus dual-pass systems — attractive for large fixed-tilt fleets with predictable dry dust.

safe.title: Safe and Reliable Operation
safe.body: NYUMA rides the module frame with lab-validated PBT contact pressure — tested for micro-crack, reflectance, and ARC integrity under daily cycles.

installSection.title: How Long Does It Take to Install the Automated Solar Panel Cleaning Robots?
installSection.p1: The installation of the Solar Panel Cleaning Robot requires no modifications to the existing solar plant layout. The NYUMA Automated Solar Panel Cleaning Robot is designed to operate directly on the panel frame.
installSection.p2: NYUMA commissioning covers dock placement, PBT brush calibration, and NECTYR onboarding — typically hours to two days per array with no structural plant changes.
installSection.p3: Full-fleet rollouts across multi-block plants may take up to a week. Post-handover operation is autonomous with Taypro same-day support and remote diagnostics.
installSection.p4: TAYPRO NYUMA robots offer cost-effective and scalable solutions for solar power plants with easy installations and safe operations.

roiSection.title: What Is the ROI for Installing the Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot NYUMA?
roiSection.p1: TAYPRO's NYUMA Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot delivers a high return on investment ROI by significantly increasing power generation.
roiSection.p2BeforeLink: Also, NYUMA reduces operational cleaning costs and offers long-term financial benefits. The initial investment is quickly recovered within one year, making it an effective and sustainable investment. You can calculate the ROI on Solar Panel Cleaning Robots by using the

cycleNarrative.title: Inside a NYUMA cleaning cycle, step by step

steps.s0: ⦿ The robot initialises its sensors on the activation and performs the system checks connectivity with the central monitoring system
steps.s1: ⦿ The cleaning schedule is pre-set via a remote monitoring portal which enables fully automated operation
steps.s2: ⦿ The locomotion motor powers the movement and guides the robot along the solar panel frames
steps.s3: ⦿ The PBT brush drum spins up for a single waterless pass — counter-rotating bristles lift dry dust at 10–15 m/min without water or detergent
steps.s4: ⦿ The edge detection sensors continually scan the surroundings which prevents the robot from overshooting the panel edges
steps.s5: ⦿ Users can adjust the cleaning speed and direction from the NECTYR
steps.s6: ⦿ Once the cleaning cycle is complete, the robot returns to its docking station, where it recharges using solar energy and remains idle until the next scheduled cycle.
steps.s7: ⦿ Brush-load and battery telemetry feed fault detection — anomalies trigger a controlled stop and NECTYR notification
steps.s8: ⦿ Self-cleaning brush action and adaptive row speed keep single-pass quality uniform across the assigned array

item0.question: Who monitors NYUMA after installation?
item0.answer: After installation, the system can be remotely monitored and controlled through the NECTYR. The TAYPRO team provides technical support and troubleshooting issues.

item1.question: Does the automatic solar module cleaning system require water for cleaning?
item1.answer: No, the TAYPRO NYUMA operates using waterless cleaning technology. It uses a rotating PBT brush cloth to remove the dust and debris efficiently.

item2.question: How often does the Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot need to be maintained?
item2.answer: The TAYPRO NYUMA requires minimal maintenance due to its self-cleaning PBT brush cloth and automated fault detection system.

item3.question: When does NYUMA clean relative to plant production?
item3.answer: The robots operate after solar energy production hours to avoid power loss and optimise the cleaning efficiency.

item4.question: Does Taypro Provide AMC for Solar Panel Cleaning?
item4.answer: Yes, TAYPRO offers AMC services for its solar panel cleaning services The AMC includes regular maintenance, real-time monitoring and onsite support targeting same-day resolution to ensure continuous operation.

item5.question: Does the Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot require an external power connection?
item5.answer: No, TAYPRO's NYUMA does not require an external power connection. It is self -powered system.

item6.question: How Do You Manage the Functioning of Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robots?
item6.answer: The cleaning robots are managed through a remote monitoring portal which allows users to schedule cleaning cycles, adjust speed and monitor system performance.

item0.question: How much does an Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot like NYUMA cost?
item0.answer: Pricing for the Taypro NYUMA Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot depends on plant size, panel layout, terrain and the level of integration with your existing monitoring stack. For most utility-scale projects, the robotic cleaning investment pays back inside 12–18 months through higher generation, lower water consumption and reduced manual labour costs. Request a quote from our team to receive a site-specific cost estimate and a detailed ROI calculation.

item1.question: What is the ROI of installing an Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Robot?
item1.answer: Robotic dry cleaning typically lifts solar plant performance ratio (PR) by 4–8% on dusty sites by maintaining higher module transmissivity day-on-day. Combined with the elimination of water tankers, manual labour and unsafe night-shift cleaning, most Taypro NYUMA deployments achieve full ROI within 12–18 months and continue to compound returns through the 20-year design life of the robot.

item2.question: What is the difference between NYUMA, GLYDE, and NYUMA-X?
item2.answer: NYUMA is Taypro's single-pass PBT automatic robot for fixed-tilt utility plants. GLYDE is the dual-pass microfiber flagship for the same plant type. NYUMA-X extends the PBT platform to single-axis trackers. All share NECTYR monitoring — pick based on mounting structure and brush technology preference.

item3.question: Is robotic solar panel cleaning safe for the modules?
item3.answer: Yes. Taypro NYUMA moves only along the module frame so no load is ever applied to the glass or solar cells. The rotating PBT brush drum is non-abrasive, and every robot is independently tested for micro-crack formation, optical reflectance loss and Anti-Reflective Coating (ARC) preservation under simulated daily cleaning cycles by accredited solar-sector testing laboratories.

item4.question: How often should solar panels be cleaned with NYUMA?
item4.answer: For most Indian utility-scale plants, a daily automated cleaning cycle delivers the best performance-ratio outcome, NYUMA is designed to run every night or post-production-hours without consuming water or manpower. Sites in extremely dusty regions or near construction zones may schedule additional cycles, while sites with frequent rainfall can dynamically reduce cycles via the weather-aware scheduler in the NECTYR.

item5.question: Does Taypro NYUMA work in Indian monsoon, summer heat and dust-storm conditions?
item5.answer: Yes. NYUMA is engineered for harsh Indian utility-scale conditions: operating temperatures up to 90°C, IP65 sealed enclosure, TÜV NORD-certified IP55 protection, and field-validated under simulated sandstorm cycles of 10 g/m² per event for 12 events per year. The robot self-docks and locks securely at wind gusts of up to 180 km/hr and pauses cleaning at unsafe wind speeds in operation.

item6.question: Can NYUMA be retrofitted to existing solar plants without modifying the panels?
item6.answer: Yes. NYUMA requires no additional railings, no modification to the existing solar module mounting structure, and no external power supply. Each robot is assigned to a specific array, runs on its own lithium-ion battery and self-charging docking station, and can be deployed across different panel inclinations and seasonal tilts with minimal site disruption.

glydeVsNyuma.title: GLYDE vs NYUMA: Which Automatic Robot Fits Your Plant?
glydeVsNyuma.subtitle: Both robots are fully autonomous, waterless, and managed from NECTYR. GLYDE is Taypro's patented dual-pass microfiber flagship; NYUMA is the PBT single-pass platform for capex-efficient fixed-tilt fleets.

row0.glyde: Patented dual-pass: airflow loosens dust, microfiber completes the wipe
row0.nyuma: Single-pass counter-rotating PBT brush drum — fewer moving consumables

row1.glyde: Flagship fixed-tilt rows where maximum dust lift per cycle is the priority
row1.nyuma: Repeatable fixed-tilt blocks where PBT capex and brush economics win RFQs

row2.glyde: Dual-pass excels on mixed dry and adhesive dust layers
row2.nyuma: Ideal for dry desert dust; schedule extra cycles if cementitious soiling is heavy

row3.glyde: Self-cleaning microfiber drum with extended service intervals
row3.nyuma: UV-stable PBT brush with field-replaceable bristle cartridges

row5.nyuma: Akhadana-scale and mixed-fleet PBT automatic programmes

row7.glyde: NECTYR via LTE, Wi-Fi, RF mesh, LoRa, or LoRaWAN
row7.nyuma: NECTYR via LTE, Wi-Fi, RF mesh, LoRa, or LoRaWAN
